_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use phrases to tell people about the things we like and do not like.

_source.w: We are learning to use phrases to tell people about the things we like and do not like.
y: Sharing our preferences helps others understand who we are.
When we express what we like and dislike, we practise forming opinions.
Using phrases instead of single words helps us communicate more clearly.
eg: We practise saying 'I like painting' or 'I don't like loud noises' during sharing time.
We use visual symbols or our device to express preferences like 'I want the blue one'.
We tell a partner about our favourite food using a full phrase.
We create a likes and dislikes chart using pictures and words.
We respond to questions about preferences with phrases like 'I prefer playing outside'.
We practise politely saying 'No thanks, I don't like that' when offered something.
